evidence.scientific_clarity: All four panels present and match brief: method diagram, ablation bars, training curves, 5x5 sensitivity heatmap; narrative is coherent and readable.
evidence.typography: Script explicitly sets font.family='DejaVu Sans' (the disallowed default); no pdf.fonttype=42 set anywhere; pdffonts confirms all three fonts are Type 3, not Type 42.
evidence.palette: RdYlGn colormap used for heatmap is not colorblind-safe (red-green colorblindness affects ~8% of males); green/red used for non-directional accuracy data.
evidence.layout_density: gridspec(2,3,width_ratios=[2,1,1]) matches brief; bottom panel spans full width; hspace/wspace tuned; no gaping whitespace or label collisions observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption describes all panels and matches figure content; panel labels A-D bold upper-left; however RdYlGn palette and absence of specific numerical values would draw reviewer comments.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is 48 KB and non-trivial, but pdffonts shows Type 3 (bitmap) fonts throughout — text is not selectable or editable; pdf.fonttype=42 never set.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (48 KB), figure.png (274 KB), figure.svg (117 KB), caption.tex (1.0 KB), gen_figure.py (7.0 KB) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains each panel's purpose and includes qualitative findings ('converges faster', 'degradation confined to low-S/low-P corners') but provides no specific numerical values (e.g., exact accuracy or final loss).

overall_strengths: Complete 4-panel layout exactly matches the brief's gridspec spec, with dominant Panel A diagram, ablation bars, training curves, and full-width heatmap.
All required output files (pdf, png, svg, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) are present and non-trivial in size.
overall_weaknesses: DejaVu Sans is hardcoded as the font family and pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, resulting in Type 3 (non-editable, non-selectable) fonts in the PDF — a disqualifying flaw for journal submission.
RdYlGn colormap on the heatmap is not colorblind-safe and violates the green/red-for-direction discipline; caption lacks concrete numerical claims to anchor the scientific findings.
